message:
feat(node): add ActionableError type, structured RunDispatchReport, registry verify/gc, request lifecycle provenance, and schema migration hardening

ActionableError and user-facing error improvements (bd-3cnmb):
- New ActionableError struct in lib.rs with message, fix_command, and
  help_urls fields implementing Display and Error
- Seven helper functions producing ActionableErrors for specific failure
  paths: missing signing key, missing trust registry, blocked preflight,
  missing trust card, registry publish without key, unavailable runtimes
- DispatchResolutionError::RequestedRuntimeUnavailable now wraps
  ActionableError with fix commands pointing to nodejs.org/bun.sh

Engine dispatcher refactor (bd-3ewxi):
- New CapturedProcessOutput and RunDispatchReport types capturing stdout,
  stderr, timing, exit code, signal, and telemetry from engine execution
- dispatch_run() returns structured RunDispatchReport instead of bare ()
- Process execution switches from .status() to .output() for capture

Registry verify/gc subcommands (bd-1mdlj):
- RegistryCommand::Verify and RegistryCommand::Gc CLI subcommands
- Signing key changed from required PathBuf to Option<PathBuf> with
  ActionableError guidance when missing

Config additions (bd-3ewxi):
- observability.max_receipts field with FRANKEN_NODE_OBSERVABILITY_MAX_RECEIPTS
  env var support, merge logic, and validation (rejects Some(0))

Request lifecycle provenance (bd-2fqyv.5.4):
- RequestLifecycleProvenance struct capturing transport boundary, listener
  ownership, endpoint group, route path, and perf baseline status
- ControlPlaneService records provenance on each request dispatch
- Seven contract tests validating runtime output matches JSON artifacts

Schema migration hardening (bd-2fqyv.6.2):
- plan_replay_proven() rewritten from O(n*m) reverse-scan to suffix-match
  for correctness: verifies last N journal entries match plan steps
- step_already_applied() now checks only latest journal entry with full
  version_from/version_to/checksum verification
- Four new tests: hash divergence fail-closed, journal tail tampering
  detection, legacy prefix tolerance, receipt byte determinism

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
